question_text: A sharp pin has a point with an area of 0.001 mm². A person pushes the pin with a force of 10 N. A flat rubber pad has an area of 100 cm² and exerts the same 10 N force. Calculate the pressure from both the pin and the pad, and explain why the pin is more dangerous despite using the same force.
correct_answer: Pin pressure: 10 N ÷ 0.000001 m² = 10,000,000 Pa (10 MPa). Pad pressure: 10 N ÷ 0.01 m² = 1000 Pa. The pin creates 10,000 times greater pressure, which can puncture skin and tissue.
explanation: Pressure depends on both force and area. The same force concentrated on a tiny point (pin) creates enormous pressure, while the same force spread over a large area (pad) creates much lower pressure. High pressure can break through materials; low pressure cannot.
